sourceCandidates.title: Hamilton County Parcel Polygons
sourceCandidates.description:
This layer displays the property boundaries of Hamilton County. Information regarding each property can be found on the Hamilton Auditor's County, by entering the owner's name, the parcel ID, or the street address. The auditor's website provides regarding the tax district, the school district, the owner name and address, the land use, the appraisal information, the assessed value, and the property description, amongst other information.

In order to perform a parcel search on Hamilton's County Auditor's website, the parcel ID should be entered without the first "0", or use the Auditor Parcel ID.

For more information, visit: https://wedge1.hcauditor.org/ or https://cagis.hamilton-co.org/CagisOnline/.

This layer doesn't display when zoomed out beyond 1:4,800.

sourceCandidates.title: Categorical Zoning Changes
sourceCandidates.description: The Categorical Changes shown on this map are areas where the major zoning classifications changed during the course of the City of Knoxville Zoning Ordinance Update (a.k.a., "Recode Knoxville"). Examples of Categorial changes include Residential to Commercial and Industrial to Office. A change in zoning from one type of commercial to another type of commercial is not included.

sourceCandidates.title: Groundbreakers Data
sourceCandidates.description:
What is Groundbreakers?

Groundbreakers is an information resource for citizens, property managers, appraisers, developers, investors, and others interested in learning more about current development activity in Knox County.


The application provides a detailed inventory of major building and renovation projects, including those that are currently under construction, proposed for development in the very near future, or recently completed.


Types

Project types reported in Groundbreakers include apartment, assisted living, commercial, industrial/warehouse, medical, mixed use, retail, and special use.


Data Sources

MPC collects information for Groundbreakers from several sources: building permits issued by Knox County, City of Knoxville, and Town of Farragut; development applications approved by the Planning Commission; local news reports; and field review.


Time Coverage

The active inventory presented in Groundbreakers includes projects that have been announced and/or completed in the past five years. Each year, we will archive projects that are more than five years old.


With Groundbreakers we strive to deliver an accurate, comprehensive, and timely inventory of development projects. If we’ve missed something or our information is not quite right, please contact:

Aubrey Weiland

aubrey.weiland@knoxplanning.org

865-215-3832


Nick Schoenborn

865-215-3805

sourceCandidates.title: Knoxville - Knox County Zoning
sourceCandidates.description: The location and boundaries of the zoning districts established by the Code of Ordinances of Knoxville and Knox County, TN are shown and maintained by Knoxville-Knox County Planning under the direction of its Executive Director. The zoning GIS layer constitutes the City of Knoxville’s Official Zoning Map and is incorporated into, and the same is made a part of the Code of Ordinances by reference. This data is updated monthly through actions of the Knox County Commission and the City of Knoxville. Check back frequently to download the latest data or consider using the REST service to gain access to the latest features.Fields OBJECTID (alias: OBJECTID): Stable, unique value for each zoning district in a GUID format ZONE1 (alias: ZONE1): Base zoning district code ZONE2 (alias: ZONE2): Overlay district code AREA_ACRES (alias: AREA_ACRES): Calculated acreage of a zoning district HIGH_DENSITY (alias: HIGH_DENSITY): Maximum dwelling units per acre allowed in a zoning district CONDITIONS (alias: CONDITIONS): Planning file number for a zoning district with specific conditions FORM_DIST (alias: Form District): Name of form district FORM_CORR (alias: Form Corridor): Name of form corridor FORM_DESCR (alias: Form Description): Form district description FORM_CODE_PDF (alias: Form Code PDF): URL to more information about a form district or corridor ZONE_TYPE (alias: ZONE_TYPE): Type of zoning district (e.g. City of Knoxville, Knox County, Form District)

sourceCandidates.title: CAD eCitations20240301
sourceCandidates.description:
This map shows the stops made by THP troopers. The data is collected using a Computer-aided Dispatch (CAD) system and citation codes.

NOTE: This map application requires a username and password for access.

Five different 10-codes used while troopers are on patrol are shown in separate feature class layers.
  • 10-45 = Auto wreck with property damage
  • 10-46 = Auto wreck with injury
  • 10-70 = Dead On Arrival (DOA)
  • 10-80 = Stationary observation
  • 10-81 = Stopping violator
Each layer's pop-up contains:
  • The THP 10-code and its meaning
  • The eCitation ID
  • The date and time of the event
Four different basemap options:
  • OpenStreetMap
  • Imagery with Labels
  • Light Gray Canvas
  • Dark Gray Canvas
There are nine bookmarks:
  • State of Tennessee
  • Chattanooga, TN
  • Columbia, TN
  • Cookeville, TN
  • Jackson, TN
  • Johnson City, TN
  • Knoxville, TN
  • Memphis, TN
  • Nashville, TN
Additionally, there are county and city names and boundaries.

There are three PCA Attribute Expressions used to generate the pop-ups:
//EventDateTime switched to "MM/DD/YYYY hh:mm" format {expression/expr0}
var aMonth, aDay, aYear, anHour, aMinute, aDate, aTime
aMonth = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 5, 2)
aDay = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 8, 2)
aYear = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 0, 4)
anHour = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 11, 2)
aMinute = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 14, 2)
aDate = Concatenate([aMonth,aDay,aYear], '/')
aTime = Concatenate([anHour,aMinute], ':')
Concatenate([aDate,aTime], ' ')

//EventDate {expression/expr1}
var aMonth, aDay, aYear, aDate
aMonth = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 5, 2)
aDay = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 8, 2)
aYear = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 0, 4)
aDate = Concatenate([aMonth,aDay,aYear], '/')
aDate

//EventTime {expression/expr2}
var anHour, aMinute, aTime
anHour = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 11, 2)
aMinute = Mid($feature.EventDateTime, 14, 2)
aTime = Concatenate([anHour,aMinute], ':')
aTime

sourceCandidates.title: Do All Chattanooga Residents Have Equal Access to Parks?
sourceCandidates.description:
Public parks are a foundational community resource. But walkable access to them is not evenly distributed. This analysis begins with a geographic question: which Chattanooga census tracts have walkable access to a public park within a quarter mile or half mile, and where are the gaps most concentrated across Hamilton County?

The second question is an equity question: does household income predict park access? Are lower-income residents more likely to live in tracts without walkable park access, or does the data tell a more complicated story?

Census tracts shaded in grey indicate limited walkable park access, meaning the tract centroid falls outside a quarter mile or half mile of any public park. Green buffer zones show the walkability thresholds around each park. The gaps are concentrated in the rural northern tracts and in clusters within the urban core.

sourceCandidates.title: TN Property Viewer
sourceCandidates.description:
Property map viewer for the State of Tennessee that covers 85 of the 95 counties in Tennessee.
This application allows for searching and displaying property ownership and location information for 87 counties in Tennessee. It is designed to work in concert with the Real Estate Assessment Data site operated by the Comptroller of the Treasury. The following counties are not available in this application but can be found on their own internet sites: Bradley, Davidson (Metro Nashville), Hamilton (Chattanooga), Knox (Knoxville), Montgomery (Clarksville), Rutherford(Murfreesboro), Shelby (Memphis), Sumner, Unicoi, and Williamson.

  • OBJECTID (alias: OBJECTID): Stable, unique value for each zoning district in a GUID format
  • ZONE1 (alias: ZONE1): Base zoning district code
  • ZONE2 (alias: ZONE2): Overlay district code
  • AREA_ACRES (alias: AREA_ACRES): Calculated acreage of a zoning district
  • HIGH_DENSITY (alias: HIGH_DENSITY): Maximum dwelling units per acre allowed in a zoning district
  • CONDITIONS (alias: CONDITIONS): MPC file number for a zoning district with specific conditions
  • FORM_DIST (alias: Form District): Name of form district
  • FORM_CORR (alias: Form Corridor): Name of form corridor
  • FORM_DESCR (alias: Form Description): Form district description
  • FORM_CODE_PDF (alias: Form Code PDF): URL to more information about a form district or corridor
  • ZONE_TYPE (alias: ZONE_TYPE): Type of zoning district (e.g. City of Knoxville, Knox County, Form District)

sourceCandidates.title: TN Property Viewer
sourceCandidates.description:
Property map viewer for the State of Tennessee that covers 85 of the 95 counties in Tennessee.
This application allows for searching and displaying property ownership and location information for 87 counties in Tennessee. It is designed to work in concert with the Real Estate Assessment Data site operated by the Comptroller of the Treasury. The following counties are not available in this application but can be found on their own internet sites: Bradley, Davidson (Metro Nashville), Hamilton (Chattanooga), Knox (Knoxville), Montgomery (Clarksville), Rutherford(Murfreesboro), Shelby (Memphis), Sumner, Unicoi, and Williamson.

sourceCandidates.title: Skai Tornado Workshop Map
sourceCandidates.description: OCI's Skai Tornado Workshop recently conducted a comprehensive demonstration focused on the Nashville, TN tornado event from March 2020. This work highlighted sophisticated model predictions that encompassed a range of key insights. This included building damage estimations, comparative satellite imagery taken before and after the disaster, a juxtaposition of the tornado's forecasted path versus its actual tract, areas of interest (AOI) identification, and a meticulously detailed density heatmap representing the most severely damaged structures within the affected zone. The demo underscored OCI's commitment to leveraging cutting-edge technology in enhancing disaster response and management.
